statement.markdown: Given a string sequence, implement a stack to check if the expression is balanced and evaluate the expression. If the expression is balanced, evaluate it and return the final value. A valid expression consists of integers and the four basic arithmetic operations (+, -, *, /). Each expression starts with an integer followed by zero or more operators and operands. The expression is balanced if every opening parenthesis is closed and every operator is preceded by an operand. Evaluate the expression from left to right.
statement.inputFormat: A string sequence representing an expression
statement.outputFormat: An integer representing the result of the expression
statement.notes: The expression is assumed to be well-formed, i.e., valid arithmetic operations are performed and the operator precedence is applied

examples.explanation: The expression is evaluated from left to right: (2+3)*4 = 20, then 20*4 = 80, but we return the first value of the first eval which is 14.

hints.text: Handle the case when the operator is not preceded by an operand

editorial.title: Infinite Loop in Stack Implementation Solution
editorial.intuition: A stack can be used to keep track of the operands and operators in the expression. The stack should pop operators when an operand is encountered, and the operator should be applied to the current operand and the top operand on the stack.
editorial.bruteForce: A brute-force approach would involve parsing the expression using a recursive descent parser or a shunting-yard algorithm and then applying the operators to the operands
editorial.betterSolution: A stack can be used to keep track of the operators and operands in the expression
editorial.optimalSolution: The stack can be used to keep track of the operators and operands in the expression, and the expression can be evaluated from left to right
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The stack-based approach ensures that every opening parenthesis is closed and every operator is preceded by an operand
editorial.dryRun: The stack-based approach ensures that every opening parenthesis is closed and every operator is preceded by an operand, and the expression can be evaluated from left to right
